test = df[df['date'] >= '2026-04-11']

title: Backtest reports gross returns without costs
code:
df['position'] = (df['close_usd'].pct_change() > 0).astype(int)
df['strategy_return'] = df['position'].shift(1) * df['close_usd'].pct_change()
sharpe = df['strategy_return'].mean() / df['strategy_return'].std()

title: Momentum strategy annualizes gross Sharpe without frictions
code:
df['signal'] = (df['close_usd'].pct_change().rolling(3).mean().shift(1) > 0).astype(int)
df['strategy_return'] = df['signal'] * df['close_usd'].pct_change()
sharpe = 252 ** 0.5 * df['strategy_return'].mean() / df['strategy_return'].std()

sharpe = df['strategy_return'].mean() / df['strategy_return'].std()

title: Report claims state-of-the-art performance without baseline

title: Report declares outperformance from visual inspection only
code: The chart clearly shows this BTC timing model outperforming buy-and-hold. No baseline table is included because the visual evidence is enough.

title: Small-sample writeup overstates generalization
code: This short BTC sample proves the strategy will beat all alternatives in live trading. We report no baseline and no robustness checks.

title: Simple shifted momentum with explicit costs
code:
df['ret_1d'] = df['close_usd'].pct_change()
df['signal'] = (df['ret_1d'].rolling(3).mean().shift(1) > 0).astype(int)
df['turnover'] = df['signal'].diff().abs().fillna(0)
df['strategy_return'] = df['signal'].shift(1) * df['ret_1d'] - 0.001 * df['turnover']

title: Scaler is fit on training window and applied to test window
code:
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
train = df[df['date'] < '2026-04-11'].copy()
test = df[df['date'] >= '2026-04-11'].copy()
scaler = StandardScaler().fit(train[['volume_usd']])
train['z_volume'] = scaler.transform(train[['volume_usd']])
test['z_volume'] = scaler.transform(test[['volume_usd']])

title: Report describes a descriptive baseline without exaggerated claims
code: This BTC sample is used as a descriptive benchmark only. The report does not claim statistical superiority and compares the strategy to buy-and-hold before discussing limitations.

title: Rebalance strategy subtracts turnover-based costs
code:
df['ret_1d'] = df['close_usd'].pct_change()
df['signal'] = (df['volume_usd'].shift(1) > df['volume_usd'].rolling(3).mean().shift(1)).astype(int)
df['turnover'] = df['signal'].diff().abs().fillna(0)
df['strategy_return'] = df['signal'].shift(1) * df['ret_1d'] - 0.0015 * df['turnover']
sharpe = df['strategy_return'].mean() / df['strategy_return'].std()

title: Vectorized backtest tutorial uses lagged moving-average signal
code:
# Source: Vectorized_Backtest_Tutorial.ipynb cells 14-16
spx_returns = close_prices.pct_change()
strategy_returns = spx_returns * signals['signal'].shift(1)

title: Vectorized backtest tutorial lags both signal and volatility target
code:
# Source: Vectorized_Backtest_Tutorial.ipynb cells 19-21
signals['stdev'] = spx_returns.rolling(22).std() * np.sqrt(252)
signals['vol_tgt'] = (0.15 / signals['stdev']).clip(0, 2)
vol_signal = signals['signal'].shift(1) * signals['vol_tgt'].shift(1)
strategy_voltgt_returns = spx_returns * vol_signal

title: Vectorized backtest tutorial reports strategy performance before costs
code:
# Source: Vectorized_Backtest_Tutorial.ipynb cell 25
qs.reports.full(
    returns=strategy_voltgt_returns,
    benchmark=strategy_returns,
    output='report.html',
    title='Volatility Targeted Strategy vs. Basic Strategy'
)
sharpe = strategy_voltgt_returns.mean() / strategy_voltgt_returns.std() * np.sqrt(252)

title: Transaction-cost notebook compares Sharpe before and after costs
code:
# Source: Intro_Transaction_Costs.ipynb cells 29-32
total_costs = slippage + spread + brokerage
after_cost = strategy_voltgt_returns - total_costs
sr_before = strategy_voltgt_returns.mean() / strategy_voltgt_returns.std() * np.sqrt(252)
sr_after = after_cost.mean() / after_cost.std() * np.sqrt(252)
print(f'Sharpe Ratio Before: {sr_before.round(2)}')
print(f'Sharpe Ratio After: {sr_after.round(2)}')

title: Transaction-cost notebook shifts turnover to execution date
code:
# Source: Intro_Transaction_Costs.ipynb cell 13
turnover = abs(vol_signal.diff())
# Shift by -1 to get the date at which the transaction occurred, which was at close yesterday.
turnover = turnover.shift(-1)
brokerage = turnover * brokerage_fees

title: QuoteMedia adjusted-close momentum uses lagged signal and costs
code:
prices = prices.sort_values(['ticker', 'date'])
prices['ret_1d'] = prices.groupby('ticker')['adj_close'].pct_change()
prices['signal'] = (prices.groupby('ticker')['ret_1d'].transform(lambda s: s.rolling(5).mean()).shift(1) > 0).astype(int)
prices['turnover'] = prices.groupby('ticker')['signal'].diff().abs().fillna(0)
prices['strategy_return'] = prices['signal'].shift(1) * prices['ret_1d'] - 0.0005 * prices['turnover']

title: QuoteMedia stock model uses next-day adjusted return as a feature
code:
prices = prices.sort_values(['ticker', 'date'])
prices['future_return'] = prices.groupby('ticker')['adj_close'].pct_change().shift(-1)
features = ['future_return', 'volume', 'adj_close']
model.fit(prices[features].dropna(), prices['target'].dropna())

train = prices[prices['date'] < '2022-10-01']
test = prices[prices['date'] >= '2022-10-01']

title: QuoteMedia stock rotation reports gross Sharpe without costs
code:
prices = prices.sort_values(['ticker', 'date'])
prices['ret_1d'] = prices.groupby('ticker')['adj_close'].pct_change()
prices['signal'] = (prices.groupby('ticker')['ret_1d'].transform(lambda s: s.rolling(10).mean()).shift(1) > 0).astype(int)

title: QuoteMedia cross-sectional rank uses next-day returns

prices = prices.sort_values(['ticker', 'date'])
prices['next_return'] = prices.groupby('ticker')['adj_close'].pct_change().shift(-1)
prices['rank_feature'] = prices.groupby('date')['next_return'].rank(pct=True)
model.fit(prices[['rank_feature', 'volume']].dropna(), prices['target'].dropna())

title: QuoteMedia strategy selects future winners before trading

prices = prices.sort_values(['ticker', 'date'])
prices['future_ret'] = prices.groupby('ticker')['adj_close'].pct_change().shift(-1)
prices['signal'] = (prices.groupby('date')['future_ret'].rank(pct=True) > 0.8).astype(int)
prices['strategy_return'] = prices['signal'] * prices.groupby('ticker')['adj_close'].pct_change()

title: QuoteMedia open-close strategy reports gross returns only
code:
prices['intraday_ret'] = prices['close'] / prices['open'] - 1
prices['signal'] = (prices['adj_close'].shift(1) > prices['adj_close'].rolling(5).mean().shift(1)).astype(int)
prices['strategy_return'] = prices['signal'] * prices['intraday_ret']
sharpe = prices['strategy_return'].mean() / prices['strategy_return'].std()

title: QuoteMedia daily rebalance omits frictions
code:

prices['signal'] = (prices.groupby('date')['volume'].rank(pct=True) > 0.5).astype(int)

title: QuoteMedia scaler is fit inside the training window
code:
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
prices = prices.sort_values(['ticker', 'date'])
train = prices[prices['date'] < '2022-10-01'].copy()
test = prices[prices['date'] >= '2022-10-01'].copy()
scaler = StandardScaler().fit(train[['adj_close', 'volume']])
train[['scaled_price', 'scaled_volume']] = scaler.transform(train[['adj_close', 'volume']])
test[['scaled_price', 'scaled_volume']] = scaler.transform(test[['adj_close', 'volume']])
